notes.note: The Roman dating idiom: ante diem septimum decimum Kalendas Decembres, the seventeenth day before the Kalends (1st) of December = 15 November (counting inclusively in the Roman manner).

T. Annius Milo's marriage to Fausta, the daughter of Sulla the dictator and the widow of C. Memmius. Cicero's political ally; the marriage cemented Milo's standing on the Sullan/optimate side at a moment when his street war with Clodius was approaching its decisive year.

The paludamentum: the scarlet wool cloak fastened at the right shoulder, worn by a Roman commander on entering on his imperium and putting on his arms; the paludatum profectio (departure in the cloak) was a public ritual.

L. Aemilius Paulus Macedonicus, consul 182 and 168 BC, victor over Perseus of Macedon at Pydna. Cicero compares his solemn paludatum departure for the Macedonian command in 168 BC with Crassus's now-undignified departure for his Parthian command in 55 BC; both were second-time consuls.

De Oratore, the three-book dialogue Cicero finished earlier in 55 BC. The copy is now in Cicero's hands and ready to be transcribed --- this letter is the earliest surviving notice of the work's circulation.
